intake.user_request: Dogfood scan of coderef-core revealed two gaps: 1) 1,883 elements have missing headers (files that never had --source-headers written); 2) only 14% layer coverage (347/2,426 elements) because layer/capability are self-referential — the scanner reads @layer from headers but headers with no @layer stay empty indefinitely without an external seed.

classification.plan_type_rationale: Two independent deliverables: Phase 1 is a mechanical first-time header pass (low risk, no logic changes); Phase 2 is a seeding strategy decision (path-inference or annotation tooling). No public API changes. Hard stop between phases to review layer coverage results.

in_scope: Run populate-coderef --source-headers (no --overwrite) to write first-time headers for the 1,883 missing-header elements
Verify header count reaches near-full coverage after the pass
Assess and implement a path-inference strategy to seed @layer/@capability for files with no prior annotation
Verify layer coverage improves measurably after seeding
out_of_scope: LLM enrichment via Lloyd (separate concern, separate WO)
Changes to the staleness detection logic (shipped in WO-FIX-EXPORTS-RAWEXPORTS-STALENESS-MISMATCH-001)
Any changes to the scanner's element-extraction logic

constraints: Phase 1 (first-time header write) must not use --overwrite-headers — only write files that have no existing header
Path-inference rules must derive from file path patterns only (no LLM calls)
Layer enum must come from STANDARDS/layers.json — no hardcoded values
